title: The Night We Lost the Sky
blurb: First contact with the Scourge out of the void is a space battle, and humanity loses it — not ready, too many. The orbital and sky defense collapses over a world that does not yet know it is already falling. The orbital relays the Skyhook still hunts were seeded in this sky.

title: Landfall
blurb: The Scourge makes landfall and the world falls. The first breaches root groundside — physical, entrenched nests punched in from the void that never stop vomiting horde.

title: The Old World Ends
blurb: In the months after Zero Day, cities are consumed and the fleets are gone. Survivors scatter down the only roads left — the lanes. The old world ends here.

title: The Delta Drowns
blurb: When the world falls, the water finishes what the Scourge started: a river-mouth city's storm-walls fail and the streets go under. What survives in the flooded fen learns to live scattered, silent, and dark — the holdout that will be called the Hush.

title: The Schism
blurb: The survivors split by doctrine, not enmity. The remnant army holds and rebuilds as the Wardens — humanity's closest thing to a state — while a zealot order breaks away as the Pyre, convinced that holding is just a slower death and the only cure is fire.

title: The Pact Is Sworn
blurb: After the rivalry nearly costs a holdout its walls, both sides swear the Pact: whatever the feud, humans stand together the instant a breach opens. The feud is exiled to sanctioned arenas like Ashgate's arena district — never the field.

title: The Repeater Secret
blurb: The Resistance fortifies the lanes and learns the Scourge's secret: cutting its repeaters severs it from the Choir, leaving the horde feral — blind, dumb, instinct-only. The war becomes a fight to fragment the signal.

title: The Wall at Ashgate
blurb: The Wardens make the foundry city their capital — not for walls it never had, but for its output and the three lanes that converge there — and seal the eastern approach under a rampart of towers, kill-corridors, and artillery stacked out of a decade of holding. Above it, the Spire's guns keep the one record that matters: it has never fallen.

title: The Lanes Go Dark
blurb: The Scourge cuts the lanes and the comms between holdouts, and the corridors between them become the Hollow Lanes — severed, pylon-seeded, held by no one. Lone runners carry the message on foot, faster than the horde can close, because a severed holdout is a dead holdout.

title: The Ninety-Second Window
blurb: Warden signals crews at Holdout Nine time the Choir's response: about ninety seconds from first contact inside a repeater-pylon's radius to both ends of the road being walled. The Wardens log it as a response window. The couriers turn it into a dare.

title: The First Descents
blurb: The first Purgers go into the breaches to sever nodes and burn the sources from the inside — most do not come back. Runs muster at the Pyre Gate, go down Pyre Road, cross the Maw, and end where the names run out: Perdition.

title: The Wells Go Pact
blurb: The Pyre come back from the first descents with empty cauterizer tanks and people still moving inside their armor; the Wardens say the grid cannot spare the draw. The argument lasts until the next breach horn — after that, the Cinder Wells become Pact ground, Warden meters on one wall and Pyre tank racks on the other.

title: Climbing the Breach-Ships
blurb: Pyre saboteurs climb the living breach-ships beached on Cinder Flats to ignite their cores and sever the local node. The biggest of them grew into the earth instead of just sitting on it — the Pyre stopped calling it a ship and named it the Rothulk.

title: The Partial Sever
blurb: Six Purgers go down past the Maw toward Perdition's edge; one comes up, three days late, tanks empty and visor scoured grey — the only return on record. The Pyre log it as a partial sever and list the lane that stayed quiet for a year after, which down there counts as a victory.

title: The Listeners Emerge
blurb: A heresy spreads: that the Choir could be understood and steered rather than only fought. Hunted by Pyre and Wardens alike, the Listeners keep severed nodes alive — and listen.
